signals.reasoning: Cover the live short immediately. The position is already losing, price and open interest are still rising together, and liquidation distance is only about 4.2%, which is too tight to defend under an active veto regime.
BTC remains structurally firm, but the event veto blocks any fresh long and there is no clean short edge either.
ETH has mild trend-supported strength, but under veto it is still watch-only rather than deployable.
SOL still looks more like a short-covering bounce than a clean directional continuation, so I would not force either side.
STBL remains one of the cleaner bearish fades because funding is still strongly positive, crowding is high, and the move already looks expensive rather than fresh.
This is too illiquid and too wide to justify a serious new trade, especially with veto active.
XPL is moving up, but there is not enough crowding for a high-quality fade and veto blocks treating it as a chase long.
ENA is still one of the better secondary short candidates because funding is positive and the structure looks like an early rollover rather than a fully broken move.
WLD has upside momentum but not enough crowding or signal quality to override the event brake.
GRIFFAIN already looks like a short-covering bounce with very thin liquidity, which makes it a poor fresh trade in either direction.
VVV is strong and extended, but veto keeps it in watch-only territory rather than a chase long.
